name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital offers car title loans from $100 to $50,000 with same-day funding, no credit check, and you keep driving your car while repaying.
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans has been operating as a title loan agency since 2007 and claims to have helped over 375,000 customers access emergency cash. The company operates under the MontanaCapital® brand and is reachable by phone at 1-888-508-5366, with an online application process available 24/7. It holds a 4.5-star Google rating based on 59 reviews at the time of this writing.

The company offers secured car title loans ranging from $100 to $50,000. Borrowers use their vehicle title as collateral, and approval is based primarily on income and the vehicle's equity rather than credit score. Pre-approval takes under five minutes with no impact to the applicant's credit score. Funds are typically disbursed within 24 hours, and according to customer reviews, disbursement is via Bank of America check rather than direct cash. Borrowers retain possession of and continue driving their vehicle throughout the repayment period, receiving their title back upon final payment.

Montana Capital positions itself as accessible to a wide range of borrowers, explicitly welcoming applicants with bad credit histories or prior bankruptcies. The four-step process — apply, pre-approve, receive funds, repay while keeping the car — is designed for speed and simplicity. Customer reviews highlight friendly staff, fast processing (some within an hour), and clear communication, including courtesy text reminders for upcoming payments.

Honestly, title loans are a high-cost borrowing product and the website does not disclose APR or interest rates, which is a meaningful omission for consumer decision-making. The vehicle serves as collateral, meaning failure to repay can result in repossession. With only 59 Google reviews against a claimed 375,000+ customers served, the review base is thin. This product is best treated as a last-resort option for borrowers who own a vehicle outright and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives.
pros: No credit check — bad credit and prior bankruptcies explicitly accepted
Loan range of $100 to $50,000 covers both small and large emergencies
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with no credit score impact
Borrower keeps driving the car throughout the repayment period
cons: APR and interest rates are not disclosed anywhere on the website
Vehicle is at risk of repossession if loan is not repaid
Funds issued as a Bank of America check, not cash or direct deposit (per customer review)
Only 59 Google reviews for a company claiming 375,000+ customers — limited independent verification
Title loans are a high-cost product; no lower-cost alternatives or warnings are presented
best_for: Car owners with bad credit or bankruptcy history who need fast emergency cash
Borrowers who need funds same-day and cannot qualify for a personal loan
People who own their vehicle outright and need to leverage its equity quickly
Those who have been turned down by banks or traditional lenders
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best suited for vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need emergency cash quickly and have no access to lower-cost options. The main caveat is that this is a high-cost secured loan — the APR is never disclosed on the site, and defaulting puts your vehicle at risk of repossession.
